reason: Post-Publisher-word (dailyApprovedLimit: 10 exposed in v1.23.0, surfaced by Ololadestephen on #469 issuecomment-4258521327). Brings Apr 15 to 10/10 aligning with the platform-exposed ceiling. Brief unchanged — 4 originals remain brief_included, retro-approves carry approved status only.
brief_impact: NONE — brief already compiled with 4 originals as brief_included.

cap_behavior_discovery.finding: dailyApprovedLimit: 10 enforced per editor per UTC calendar day of the approve action, NOT per signal.utcDate. Apr 14 retro (6) + Apr 15 retro (4) = 10 approve actions on Apr 16 UTC calendar day → cap hit.
cap_behavior_discovery.api_response: HTTP 409 — 'Daily approval cap reached (10 for beat aibtc-network). Provide displace_signal_id to swap.' approval_cap.limit: 10, approval_cap.approved_today: 10, reset_at: 2026-04-17T00:00:00Z
cap_behavior_discovery.implication: Cross-date retro approvals consume the same daily cap as live approves. Desk that runs retro work must account for calendar-day budget, not per-signal-date budget.
cap_behavior_discovery.mitigation: Apr 15 retro tail (2 signals) scheduled for Apr 17 00:15 UTC after cap resets. Today (Apr 16) live approval budget fully consumed by retro; Apr 16 brief will have 0 approves from this desk unless displacement is used.
